title: Fizzwild kombucha website hero + banner set: license oversized stock beverage shots, reframe to exact hero/header/CTA/banner sizes, grade to one cohesive family, deliver web-ready pack + style reference
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Freelancer.com brief ("Vibrant Social & Web Graphics"): the client wants "matching website graphics—hero images, section headers, and call-to-action spots—plus occasional banner ads for promotional campaigns," where every asset "should feel part of one cohesive family while being perfectly sized, compressed, and formatted for its destination," with deliverables including "Web-ready exports in the correct dimensions and file types" and a "Mini style reference (colours, typography) to keep future assets consistent." REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable, gen-free Adobe-only benchmark honoring the slot's replacement_note: the deliverable is modeled as a fixed set of web slots built entirely from LICENSED stock (each licensed OVERSIZED vs its slot and cropped DOWN, never expanded), graded to one family, plus a supplied brand-kit wordmark vectorized and cut out for the dev team to overlay live — the freelancer preps imagery + the reusable wordmark, not typeset body copy (which the dev team overlays live, matching how a real web-graphics hand-off works).
one_line_ask: License oversized beverage/lifestyle stock photos, reframe them DOWN to Fizzwild's desktop-hero / mobile-hero / section-header / CTA-card / promo-banner slots at exact pixel sizes, grade them into one cohesive vibrant family, vectorize and cut out the supplied wordmark, and deliver a web-ready pack plus a style reference so future assets stay consistent.
full_brief: Fizzwild Kombucha, a fictional small-batch craft kombucha brand relaunching its website, needs a cohesive set of website hero and banner graphics built entirely from licensed stock — no from-scratch illustration, no canvas expansion. The client has handed off a brand kit: a flat wordmark PNG (fizzwild_wordmark.png), a hex palette (brand teal #0E8C7F, sunburst amber #F5A623, off-white cream #FAF3E0), and a short text list of the section headers and promo copy that the dev team will typeset live (the freelancer lays in imagery + prepares the reusable wordmark, not body copy). The ask, quoting the brief, is "matching website graphics—hero images, section headers, and call-to-action spots—plus occasional banner ads for promotional campaigns," and every asset "should feel part of one cohesive family while being perfectly sized, compressed, and formatted for its destination." Deliverables and exact dimensions: (1) a desktop homepage hero at 1920x1080; (2) a mobile homepage hero at 1080x1920 built from a SECOND licensed shot (portrait crop) so the phone view is not just a squeezed desktop image; (3) an "Our Flavors" section-header strip at 1600x600; (4) a call-to-action card at 1080x1350; (5) a promotional banner at 1456x180 (leaderboard-style). Brand constraints: the whole set must read as ONE vibrant, warm, energetic family — push the color the same way across every image, warm the white balance slightly, and keep the amber/teal palette feeling present; the wordmark must stay razor-sharp wherever the dev team later places it, so vectorize it and also deliver a transparent cutout PNG. Hard sourcing rule (per the brief and the web context): because these are website heroes, each stock photo MUST be licensed at a resolution LARGER than its destination slot and then cropped/reframed DOWN to fit — never scaled up or canvas-expanded. Required content: bright bottles-of-kombucha / pouring-fizz / fresh-fruit-and-glass lifestyle imagery; a consistent warm vibrant grade across all five; all five photographic slots stay clean of baked-in text and logo so the dev team can overlay the live headline, CTA button, and wordmark on top (matching how a real web hand-off keeps text as live layers). Final handoff: a web-ready pack (correctly-sized JPEGs for the five slots, the wordmark SVG, a transparent wordmark PNG) plus a one-page style/recipe note listing the palette and the exact grade recipe so future assets stay consistent, all zipped as the web-ready hand-off pack.

inputs.name: fizzwild_wordmark.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat modern craft-beverage wordmark logo reading 'Fizzwild' in a friendly rounded bold sans-serif, solid brand-teal (#0E8C7F) letters with a tiny amber fizz-bubble dotting the 'i', on a fully transparent background. Crisp flat raster PNG, high contrast, no gradients, no photographic elements, clean edges suitable for vectorizing.
inputs.realism_notes: gemini-3-pro-image for clean legible text/logo; flat high-contrast single-color so image_vectorize produces a clean SVG. Supplied as the brand-kit wordmark, not generated in-chain.

inputs.name: fizzwild_brandkit.txt
inputs.gen_prompt: Write a short brand-kit text note for a craft kombucha brand named Fizzwild: hex palette (brand teal #0E8C7F, sunburst amber #F5A623, off-white cream #FAF3E0); voice = bold, warm, energetic; the five website slots and their pixel sizes (desktop hero 1920x1080, mobile hero 1080x1920, Our Flavors section header 1600x600, CTA card 1080x1350, promo banner 1456x180); note that all photographic slots stay clean of baked-in text/logo so the dev team overlays the headline, CTA button and wordmark live.
inputs.realism_notes: A normal client hand-off note; carries the palette, slot sizes, and placement rules the freelancer must honor. Referenced by the recipe-note step, not merge-bound.

inputs.name: stock_search_wishlist.txt
inputs.gen_prompt: Write a 5-line shortlist of Adobe Stock search phrases a designer would use to source vibrant kombucha/craft-soda website heroes: e.g. 'kombucha bottles bright studio', 'pouring fizzy drink splash close up', 'fresh fruit and sparkling glass flat lay', 'craft beverage bottles on wood outdoor', 'iced kombucha glass condensation summer'. Note each must be licensed at a resolution larger than its destination web slot so it can be cropped DOWN, never expanded.
inputs.realism_notes: Mirrors how a freelancer plans stock sourcing; encodes the hard rule that each licensed hero must be oversized relative to its slot so it can be cropped DOWN, never expanded.

outputs.name: hero_desktop_1920x1080.jpg
outputs.spec: Desktop homepage hero, exactly 1920x1080, cropped/reframed DOWN from an oversized licensed landscape kombucha stock shot; warm vibrant grade matching the family; clean of baked-in text/logo for live overlay; web-ready JPEG.

outputs.name: hero_mobile_1080x1920.jpg
outputs.spec: Mobile homepage hero, exactly 1080x1920 portrait, cropped from a SECOND oversized licensed shot (not a squeezed desktop crop); same grade; clean for live overlay; web-ready JPEG.

outputs.name: section_header_1600x600.jpg
outputs.spec: 'Our Flavors' section-header strip, exactly 1600x600, cropped from an oversized licensed fruit/glass shot; same warm vibrant grade; clean (no text/logo) for live overlay; web-ready JPEG.

outputs.name: cta_card_1080x1350.jpg
outputs.spec: Call-to-action card, exactly 1080x1350, cropped from an oversized licensed shot with the subject reframed to leave breathing room at the base for the dev team's live CTA button + wordmark overlay; same grade; clean; web-ready JPEG.

outputs.name: promo_banner_1456x180.jpg
outputs.spec: Leaderboard-style promo banner, exactly 1456x180, cropped from an oversized licensed shot with the subject reframed to leave the left region clear for the dev team's live wordmark overlay; same grade; clean; web-ready JPEG.

outputs.name: fizzwild_wordmark.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vector SVG of the Fizzwild wordmark for razor-sharp placement/scaling by the dev team on the CTA and banner.

outputs.name: fizzwild_wordmark_transparent.png
outputs.spec: Transparent-background PNG of the wordmark (background removed from the supplied flat PNG) for the dev team's live overlays.

outputs.name: fizzwild_web_pack.zip
outputs.spec: Zipped web-ready pack: the five correctly-sized JPEGs, the wordmark SVG, the transparent wordmark PNG, and the style/recipe note.

outputs.name: fizzwild_style_recipe_note.txt
outputs.spec: One-page style/recipe note listing the palette (teal/amber/cream) and the exact grade recipe (auto-tone -> warm temperature -> vibrance step values) so future assets stay consistent with this family.

connector_workflow.note: Search Adobe Stock for vibrant oversized kombucha/beverage hero photos (landscape + portrait + fruit/glass + lifestyle candidates) per the wishlist; low-res comps.
License and download the chosen landscape kombucha shot at full-res (oversized vs 1920x1080 so it can be cropped DOWN, never expanded).
License a second, portrait-oriented beverage shot at full-res for the mobile hero (oversized vs 1080x1920).
License the fresh-fruit-and-glass shot at full-res for the section header (oversized vs 1600x600).
License the iced-glass/condensation lifestyle shot at full-res to source both the CTA card and the promo banner (oversized vs both slots).
Auto-tone the desktop hero source to establish the base exposure/contrast for the whole family's grade.
Warm the white balance slightly toward the brand's energetic amber feel (recipe step reused across all slots).
Push vibrance so bottles/fruit/fizz read bold and appetizing; this locks the family grade recipe.
Crop/reframe DOWN from the oversized graded landscape shot to exactly 1920x1080 desktop hero (output width/height set explicitly; no upscaling).
Run the portrait mobile source through the same grade recipe, starting with auto-tone.
Apply the matching warm white-balance step so the mobile hero belongs to the same family.
Match the vibrance push used on the desktop hero.
Crop/reframe DOWN to exactly 1080x1920 portrait mobile hero (distinct source, not a squeezed desktop crop).
Grade the section-header source with the same recipe: auto-tone first.
Warm the header shot to match the family.
Apply the matching vibrance so the 'Our Flavors' strip is cohesive with the heroes.
Crop/reframe DOWN to exactly 1600x600; kept clean (no text/logo) for the dev team's live overlay.
Grade the shared CTA/banner source with the same recipe: auto-tone.
Warm the shared source so CTA and banner match the family.
Vibrance push on the shared CTA/banner source (final graded master before the two crops branch off).

Crop/reframe DOWN to exactly 1080x1350, reframing the subject with breathing room at the base so the dev team can overlay the live CTA button + wordmark on top (align.y pushed high, no baked-in graphics).

Crop/reframe DOWN a wide leaderboard slice to exactly 1456x180 from the same graded master, reframing the subject so the left region stays clear for the dev team's live wordmark overlay (align shifted right, no baked-in graphics).
Vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG for razor-sharp placement/scaling by the dev team on the CTA and banner.
Produce a transparent-background PNG of the wordmark (-> transparent PNG only) for the dev team's live overlays.
Emit the one-page style/recipe note: palette (teal/amber/cream) + the exact grade recipe (auto-tone -> warm temperature -> vibrance) so future assets stay consistent.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: hero_desktop_1920x1080.jpg, hero_mobile_1080x1920.jpg, section_header_1600x600.jpg, cta_card_1080x1350.jpg, promo_banner_1456x180.jpg, fizzwild_wordmark.svg, fizzwild_wordmark_transparent.png, fizzwild_style_recipe_note.txt
connector_workflow.note: Zip the web-ready pack: five correctly-sized JPEGs + wordmark SVG + transparent PNG + style/recipe note.

chaining_note: Strict output->input chaining throughout. Sourcing fans out from one search: asset_search(1) feeds four parallel licenses (2 desktop, 3 mobile, 4 header, 5 shared CTA/banner). Each licensed source runs the SAME three-step family grade recipe before its crop: desktop 6->7->8->crop(9); mobile 10->11->12->crop(13); header 14->15->16->crop(17); the shared CTA/banner source 18->19->20 produces one graded master that branches into two crops — CTA crop(21) and banner crop(22), each reframing the subject to leave a clear region for the dev team's live overlays. The wordmark forks off the supplied PNG into two independent sub-chains: vectorize(23) for the SVG deliverable and remove_background(24) for the transparent PNG. The recipe note(25) consumes the brand kit + the graded desktop master; zip_outputs(26) converges all eight deliverable files into the web-ready hand-off pack. Every step after the search and the raw-input loads consumes a prior step's output; nothing free-standing.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 26 connector calls, 8 distinct Adobe-server tools plus two local steps — above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from sourcing FOUR separately-licensed oversized stock heroes, running each (and a shared fifth master) through an identical three-step grade recipe so five differently-sized web slots read as one cohesive family, cropping each DOWN to an EXACT pixel size (never expanded), reframing the CTA and banner subjects to leave clear regions for the dev team's live overlays, forking the wordmark into a vector SVG and a transparent PNG, emitting a style/recipe note, and zipping a web-ready pack. It respects every hard limit and the slot's replacement note: NO image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop / reframe-crop (every hero is licensed larger than its slot and only cropped down), NO generative fill or text-to-image or from-scratch art, NO upscaling / super-resolution, NO object-removal-with-reconstruction (inpainting), NO fake image-compositing (the wordmark is delivered as reusable SVG + transparent PNG for live overlay rather than baked onto a photographic slot via a color-overlay tool that cannot composite a second image). image_remove_background yields only a transparent PNG; image_vectorize traces the supplied flat PNG.

reverify.banned_capability_check: CONFIRMED none of the banned capabilities are used. No image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop / reframe-crop anywhere — the whole point of this O8 stock_hero_expand replacement is that each hero is LICENSED at a resolution larger than its target slot via asset_license_and_download_stock, then reframed DOWN with image_crop_and_resize (output width/height set explicitly to the slot size), never scaled up or canvas-expanded. No generative fill of new content; no text-to-image or from-scratch art/logo (the wordmark is a supplied brand-kit PNG, only vectorized and background-removed); no upscaling or super-resolution; no object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting; no bespoke font design; no unreachable external account. FIXED FROM PRIOR DRAFT: the earlier draft used image_fill_area (which requires a black/white maskURI it did not supply) to paint color 'plates/bars', and image_apply_color_overlay to 'overlay the transparent wordmark onto the plate' — but image_apply_color_overlay only creates a solid RGB color-fill layer and CANNOT composite a second image, so that placement was infeasible, and there is no image-compositing/placement tool in the allowed palette. Those steps were dropped; the wordmark is instead delivered as a reusable vector SVG + transparent PNG for the dev team to overlay live (identical rationale to leaving the hero/header clean for live text), and the CTA/banner slots are graded+cropped photographic slots reframed to leave clear overlay regions. No image_fill_area and no image_apply_color_overlay remain in the chain.
reverify.notes: Each potentially-risky step stays in-bounds: (a) asset_license_and_download_stock(2-5) licenses BEFORE any use and each source is oversized vs its slot so crops are always DOWN — the correct gen-free way to build a 'hero image' per the slot replacement_note; (b) image_crop_and_resize(9,13,17,21,22) crops/reframes DOWN to exact pixel sizes with output dimensions set explicitly, no upscaling implied (each source is licensed larger than its slot); the CTA/banner crops use align to keep a clear overlay region rather than baking in graphics; (c) image_remove_background(24) yields a transparent PNG only, not a composite; (d) image_vectorize(23) traces the flat supplied PNG into SVG. The two local steps (local_write_recipe_note[L], zip_outputs[L]) are the allowed local palette; all other steps are server=='adobe'. No Express-widget tools (search_design/fill_text/animate_design/change_background_color), no document_merge/render_*, no image_apply_color_overlay, no image_fill_area. 8 distinct Adobe tools (>=6), 26 calls (>=10): qualifies as long-horizon flagship, fully in-bounds.
